entity.label: Baanah
entity.subtitle: Male
entity.description: Son of Hushai, another of Solomon’s purveyors (1 Kings 4:16).

nodes.label: Baanah
nodes.description: Son of Hushai, another of Solomon’s purveyors (1 Kings 4:16).

nodes.label: Hushai
nodes.description: Quick, “the Archite,” “the king’s friend” (1 Chr. 27:33). When David fled from Jerusalem, on account of the rebellion of Absalom, and had reached the summit of Olivet, he there met Hushai, whom he sent back to Jerusalem for the purpose of counteracting the influence of Ahithophel, who had joined the ranks of Absalom (2 Sam. 15:32, 37; 16:16-18). It was by his advice that Absalom refrained from immediately pursuing after David. By this delay the cause of Absalom was ruined, for it gave David time to muster his forces.

verses.text: Baanah the son of Hushai was in Asher and in Aloth:
